mnemonics: B-T-P-D: Batch, Time-sharing, Personal, Distributed (for the evolution of computing environments)
keyPoints: Batch Processing: Executes jobs in groups without user interaction
Time-Sharing: Allows multiple users to interact with a system concurrently via terminals
Distributed Systems: Use multiple connected computers to provide a cohesive service or resource
commonMistakes: Confusing time-sharing with multiprogramming (time-sharing is a type of multiprogramming with quick user response)
Assuming personal computing environments are isolated (modern PCs are often nodes in a larger distributed environment like the cloud)
